description: Baseline: measured current core RuboCop autocorrect gap after autoresearch scaffolding.

asi.hypothesis: Establish a deterministic baseline metric before implementing any new cop autocorrections.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement autocorrect for a low-risk token replacement cop to reduce missing count by 1.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/EndBlock by rewriting END keyword to at_exit and add corrected fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Simple keyword replacement cops can provide low-risk +1 improvements quickly.
asi.learning: RuboCop parity for this cop is a direct keyword replacement (`END` -> `at_exit`) with no structural rewrite needed.
asi.next_action_hint: Target another low-complexity token-level cop (e.g., Lint/BigDecimalNew).

description: Implement autocorrect for Lint/BigDecimalNew by removing `.new` (and leading `::` for cbase) plus corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: Small method-token removals are a reliable way to add parity quickly.
asi.learning: Using selector range removal (`.new`) works for standard calls; cbase (`::BigDecimal`) also needs `::` removal to match RuboCop output.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with single-selector replacement/remove cops in Style/Lint for incremental -1 improvements.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/StderrPuts by rewriting receiver+selector (`$stderr.puts` / `STDERR.puts`) to `warn`, with corrected fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Receiver+selector replacement cops are another low-risk class for fast parity gains.
asi.learning: Replacing range [send start..selector end] with `warn` preserves argument syntax for both parenthesized and non-parenthesized calls.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for additional one-range replacements (e.g., call-name swaps) before tackling multi-edit layout cops.

description: Implement autocorrect for Style/RedundantCurrentDirectoryInPath by removing leading `./+` from require_relative string literals and add corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: String-prefix removal cops can be added with simple byte-range deletions.
asi.learning: Removing `./+` from quoted string content via byte offsets matches existing fixtures and preserves surrounding quotes.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting Style/Lint cops with single-range substitutions before moving to layout/indentation transforms.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/ArrayJoin by rewriting `array * string` calls to `array.join(string)` and add corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: Full-node replacement is effective for simple operator-to-method migrations.
asi.learning: Replacing the full call with `<receiver_source>.join(<arg_source>)` matches fixture behavior and avoids partial-token edge cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Prioritize remaining Style cops that can be expressed as whole-call rewrites or simple selector swaps.

description: Implement autocorrect for Style/ArrayCoercion by rewriting `[*expr]` to `Array(expr)` and add corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: Single-splat array coercion cases can be safely converted with whole-node replacement.
asi.learning: For bracketed one-element arrays with splat, replacing full node with `Array(<splat_expr_source>)` matches current detection scope and fixture behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue harvesting low-complexity Style cops using source-preserving whole-node replacements.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/EnvHome by replacing `ENV['HOME']`/`ENV.fetch('HOME', nil)` forms with `Dir.home` (leaving index-or-write cases uncorrected).

asi.hypothesis: Direct expression replacement cops can still autocorrect partially even when some matched node shapes are intentionally non-correctable.
asi.learning: Safe rewrite is straightforward for call-node forms; keeping `ENV['HOME'] ||= ...` unchanged avoids invalid assignment targets while still enabling autocorrect support.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with cops where the offense range is the whole expression and replacement is a stable literal/template.

description: Implement autocorrect for Lint/IdentityComparison by rewriting `lhs.object_id ==/!= rhs.object_id` to `lhs.equal?(rhs)` / `!lhs.equal?(rhs)`, with corrected fixture coverage and research log update.

asi.hypothesis: Object-id comparison cops can be converted with receiver extraction and whole-expression replacement.
asi.learning: Using inner `.object_id` receiver source for both sides preserves ivar/lvar forms and yields clean `equal?` rewrites for both `==` and `!=`.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue targeting call-rewrite cops in Lint/Style before addressing layout-family autocorrections.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/Dir (`File.expand_path(File.dirname(__FILE__))`/`File.dirname(File.realpath(__FILE__))` -> `__dir__`), Style/SymbolLiteral (`:"foo"` -> `:foo`), and Style/FileNull (null-device string -> `File::NULL`) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Batching several low-complexity whole-node rewrites should produce a larger real metric drop per iteration without risky parser changes.
asi.learning: The dominant fast-path remains single replacement edits over offense node ranges; visitor-based cops like FileNull can autocorrect by collecting offense ranges then emitting replacements post-traversal.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::dir && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::symbol_literal && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::file_null
asi.next_action_hint: Continue mining short Style/Lint cops with one replace/remove operation before tackling Layout indentation-family corrections.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ColonMethodCall (`::` method call operator -> `.`) and Style/ClassCheck (selector rewrite between `kind_of?`/`is_a?`) with corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: Selector-level rewrites in method-call cops are high-confidence, low-risk additions that scale quickly.
asi.learning: Using exact selector/operator locations (`call_operator_loc`, `message_loc`) yields stable autocorrects without disturbing arguments/receiver formatting.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::colon_method_call && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::class_check
asi.next_action_hint: Keep harvesting selector/operator rewrite cops in Style and simple whole-node rewrite cops in Lint.

description: Add autocorrect for Security/JSONLoad (selector rewrite `load`/`restore` -> `parse`) and Lint/RegexpAsCondition (append ` =~ $_` to implicit regexp conditions) with corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: Simple selector and expression-template rewrites across Security/Lint can continue reducing the parity gap steadily.
asi.learning: For selector-focused cops, replacing only `message_loc` gives stable corrections; for match-current-line nodes, full-node replacement template (`<regexp> =~ $_`) matches RuboCop intent.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::security::json_load && cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::regexp_as_condition
asi.next_action_hint: Prioritize remaining Style/Lint cops with either selector swaps or one-shot full-expression templates before moving to structural/layout autocorrects.

description: Enable autocorrect for Security/IoMethods (replace `IO` receiver with `File`) and Lint/TopLevelReturnWithArgument (rewrite `return <arg>` to `return`) with corrected fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Remaining security/lint gaps with direct receiver or full-node rewrites can be closed quickly without touching detection logic.
asi.learning: Receiver-only replacement (`IO` -> `File`) preserves method args cleanly; visitor-based source cops can autocorrect by storing offense ranges and applying deterministic replacements post-traversal.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::security::io_methods && cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::top_level_return_with_argument
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with Style/Lint cops that expose selector/operator locations for minimal-risk edits.

description: Add autocorrect for Lint/OrAssignmentToConstant (`||=` -> `=`) and Style/PercentQLiterals (`%Q` <-> `%q` opener rewrite per configured style) with corrected fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Operator-token and percent-literal opener rewrites are low-friction additions that retain source shape while closing parity gaps.
asi.learning: Direct `operator_loc` replacement works for Prism *OrWrite nodes; for percent literals, replacing just the two-byte `%Q/%q` opener is sufficient and robust for current fixture scope.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::or_assignment_to_constant && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::percent_q_literals
asi.next_action_hint: Target more selector/operator rewrite cops and avoid deep AST restructuring until low-hanging fruit is exhausted.

description: Implement autocorrect for Style/StringMethods (selector rewrite to preferred method, default `intern` -> `to_sym`) and Style/ObjectThen (selector rewrite `yield_self` <-> `then` per enforced style), with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Config-driven selector replacement cops can be converted rapidly using `message_loc` edits while preserving call structure.
asi.learning: For style cops that only differ by method name preference, replacing selector bytes is enough and avoids complex AST rewrites.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::string_methods && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::object_then
asi.next_action_hint: Continue on style cops with message-level replacements (`intern`-like, alias method preference, keyword style toggles).

description: Add autocorrect for Lint/ToJSON (insert `(*_args)` on zero-arity `to_json` defs) and Style/BarePercentLiterals (rewrite `%Q` opener to bare `%` / `%` to `%Q` per style) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Method-signature insertion and percent-literal opener rewrites are robust, low-risk transformations for remaining Lint/Style gaps.
asi.learning: `name_loc` insertion works cleanly for def autocorrect (`to_json`); for percent literals, editing only the opener token preserves delimiters/content and matches fixture expectations.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::to_json && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::bare_percent_literals
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with style cops where corrections are pure selector/opener edits and avoid multiline structural rewrites until necessary.

description: Implement autocorrect for Style/PreferredHashMethods (selector rewrite between short/verbose hash predicates) and Style/ArrayIntersectWithSingleElement (`intersect?([x])` -> `include?(x)`) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Config-aware selector rewrites and small paired edits (selector + argument simplification) remain high-yield targets.
asi.learning: For `intersect?([elem])`, two non-overlapping edits (method selector and array-arg node) are enough for faithful correction without full-call reconstruction.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting style cops where correction can be expressed as one or two localized range edits.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/DefWithParentheses (remove empty def parens) and Style/MethodDefParentheses (add/remove method parameter parens per style) with corrected fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Definition-signature cops can be corrected with localized delimiter edits on `def` nodes.
asi.learning: For adding method parens, replacing the whitespace between method name and first param with `(` avoids `def foo (x)` spacing artifacts; delimiter-only edits remain stable.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::def_with_parentheses && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::method_def_parentheses
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with syntax-style cops where corrections are insertion/removal of small delimiter tokens around existing source ranges.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/SingleArgumentDig (`obj.dig(x)` -> `obj[x]`) and Style/RedundantArrayConstructor (`Array.new([...])`/`Array[...]`/`Array([...])` simplification) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Whole-call replacement cops with clear canonical output are still strong low-risk opportunities.
asi.learning: For redundant constructor patterns, replacing the full call with source-derived argument forms (`[...]` or literal array arg) is deterministic and fixture-stable.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::single_argument_dig && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::redundant_array_constructor
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed with other expression-level simplification cops before entering heavy layout indentation work.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/EmptyLambdaParameter (remove empty lambda parens) and Style/EmptyBlockParameter (remove empty block pipes) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Parameter-delimiter removal cops are low-risk full-range deletions that should safely increase autocorrectable overlap.
asi.learning: Using BlockParametersNode location with optional preceding-space trim produced stable corrections for both `-> () {}` and `do || ... end`/`{ || ... }` forms.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::empty_lambda_parameter && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::empty_block_parameter
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with small Style/Lint cops that are operator/selector replacements before attempting complex Layout alignment cops.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/SuperWithArgsParentheses (insert missing parens), Lint/EmptyEnsure (remove empty ensure keyword), and Style/HashLookupMethod (fetch<->[] rewrites) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Simple token/range edits on existing parsers can safely unlock another small batch of RuboCop-core autocorrectable cops without changing detection scope.
asi.learning: Super paren insertion works reliably by replacing keyword->first-arg gap with '(' and inserting ')' after last arg/block pass; empty ensure aligns with RuboCop by removing only `ensure` keyword; hash lookup conversion is stable via full-call replacement.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::super_with_args_parentheses && cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::empty_ensure && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::hash_lookup_method
asi.next_action_hint: Continue targeting low-branching Style/Lint cops around symbol/string/operator rewrites before moving into high-risk Layout alignment autocorrects.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/BooleanSymbol, including standalone `:true`/`:false` and keyword-hash `true:`/`false:` conversion to rocket form.

asi.hypothesis: Boolean-symbol replacements are deterministic and safe to implement as direct key/symbol range rewrites.
asi.learning: Using two correction modes matches RuboCop semantics: standalone symbols become boolean literals, while keyword hash labels become `:true =>` / `:false =>` to preserve symbol-key behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with low-risk style cops requiring quote/operator swaps (e.g., QuotedSymbols or RedundantPercentQ with strict guardrails).

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantPercentQ by replacing redundant `%q/%Q` string literals with quoted string literals under strict safety guards.

asi.hypothesis: Redundant percent-literal offenses can be autocorrected safely when interpolation/escape-sensitive content is excluded.
asi.learning: Whole-node replacement to `'...'` or `"..."` works for current offense patterns; keeping backslash/interpolation guards avoids semantic drift.
asi.next_action_hint: Pursue another low-complexity Style cop (likely quote/operator rewrite) while maintaining strict correction guards to avoid overfitting.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/QuotedSymbols by normalizing quoted symbol delimiters for standalone symbols and hash-key symbols when conversion is safe.

asi.hypothesis: Quoted symbol offenses can be fixed via direct delimiter swaps while preserving content and key-vs-standalone form.
asi.learning: Replacing full symbol ranges with reconstructed quoted forms (`:'x'`, `'x':`, and double-quoted inverses) passes fixtures when interpolation/escape guards remain strict.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly localized literal/operator cops; postpone layout-indent transformations until simple style/lint options are exhausted.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/UriRegexp by rewriting `URI.regexp` calls to `URI::DEFAULT_PARSER.make_regexp` (including qualified `::URI`).

asi.hypothesis: `URI.regexp` replacements are straightforward full-call rewrites and should be low-risk parity wins.
asi.learning: Receiver-preserving full replacement (`URI` or `::URI`) with optional first argument preserved matches existing fixture expectations.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with low-branching lint/style literal/operator rewrites; avoid structural transformation cops until easy wins flatten out.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ConcatArrayLiterals by rewriting `receiver.concat([..], [..])` to `receiver.push(..)` with flattened array elements.

asi.hypothesis: `concat` calls whose arguments are all array literals can be safely canonicalized to `push` with element arguments.
asi.learning: Full-call replacement with flattened array element sources produced stable fixture-correct output for single and multiple array arguments.
asi.next_action_hint: Target additional simple literal/argument-shape cops in Style/Lint before starting Layout alignment corrections.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/MinMax by replacing `[receiver.min, receiver.max]` array literals with `receiver.minmax`.

asi.hypothesis: Min/max pair arrays are canonicalizable via a direct whole-range replacement to `.minmax`.
asi.learning: Replacing the full offending array node with `{receiver}.minmax` is stable for current fixture patterns and keeps diagnostics unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue harvesting low-risk style/lint canonicalization cops; keep layout cops deferred until simple wins are exhausted.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/PercentSymbolArray by removing unnecessary leading `:` and trailing `,` inside `%i/%I` elements.

asi.hypothesis: Element-local deletions on `%i/%I` literals should safely match RuboCop behavior for colon/comma cleanup.
asi.learning: Applying per-element leading/trailing token removals yields expected normalization; corrected fixture needed `%i[name role status]` (not colon-prefixed elements) for `%i[]` form.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with conservative Lint canonicalization cops; avoid ambiguous-expression rewrites unless correction boundaries are unambiguous.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantDoubleSplatHashBraces by replacing `**{...}` keyword-hash splats with direct keyword arguments.

asi.hypothesis: For non-empty symbol-key hash literals inside keyword splats, removing `**{}` wrapper is a safe canonicalization.
asi.learning: Using first/last hash element ranges to rebuild replacement preserves argument ordering and commas while avoiding hash-rocket and empty-hash edge cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting simple keyword/argument literal cops before moving to high-complexity layout or parser-warning-driven rewrites.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantEach by removing chained redundant `.each` before `each`/`each_with_index`/`each_with_object` calls.

asi.hypothesis: Redundant chained enumerator `.each` calls can be corrected by removing the inner `.each` selector/operator span only.
asi.learning: Deleting the inner call-operator + selector range preserved surrounding chain behavior and matched fixture expectations for `each`, `each_with_index`, and `each_with_object`.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep harvesting small chain/literal canonicalization cops; defer high-risk layout alignment autocorrect work.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/UnpackFirst by converting `.unpack(...).first`/`[0]`/`at(0)`/`slice(0)` into `.unpack1(...)` and removing first-element access.

asi.hypothesis: Unpack-first chains can be safely corrected via selector swap (`unpack`->`unpack1`) plus tail-range removal.
asi.learning: Two precise edits (selector replacement + remove range from unpack-call end to outer-call end) handled both single-line and multiline chain fixtures.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly localized chain canonicalizations; keep ambiguous parser-warning cops gated behind stricter safety checks.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/LambdaCall by rewriting implicit `lambda.(...)` calls to explicit `lambda.call(...)` in default call style.

asi.hypothesis: Lambda call-style offenses are safe full-call rewrites when receiver/operator/argument text is preserved.
asi.learning: Reconstructing full call text from receiver + operator + args allows deterministic conversion from implicit call syntax to explicit `call` without touching detection logic.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with style chain canonicalization cops that can be fixed via selector/range rewrites; keep ambiguous parser-warning cops conservative.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantArrayFlatten by removing redundant `.flatten` (and optional depth arg) before `.join`/`.join(nil)`.

asi.hypothesis: Redundant flatten-before-join patterns are safe to autocorrect via direct removal of the `.flatten(...)` span.
asi.learning: Deleting from flatten call-operator start through flatten call end correctly normalized `x.flatten.join`, `x.flatten.join(nil)`, and `x.flatten(1).join` fixture cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with one-range chain simplification cops (e.g., map/set or collection canonicalizations) before layout-focused work.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/MapToSet by rewriting `map/collect { ... }.to_set` to `to_set { ... }` and removing trailing `.to_set`.

asi.hypothesis: Map-to-set chains can be corrected with two localized edits: selector swap on inner call and removal of outer `.to_set`.
asi.learning: Using a paired correction (inner method name -> `to_set`, then deleting outer call operator+selector) preserves both block and symbol block-pass forms.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with collection canonicalization cops that can be expressed as selector/range edits before entering layout-heavy territory.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/InPatternThen by replacing semicolon separator in single-line `in pattern; body` clauses with ` then`.

asi.hypothesis: In-pattern semicolon offenses can be fixed with a one-character range replacement (`;` -> ` then`) under existing multiline/then guards.
asi.learning: Using detected semicolon byte offset for targeted replacement produced stable autocorrect across literal, tuple, and match-alt pattern fixtures.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with localized token/selector rewrites in Style; defer structural multiline/layout transformations.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantConstantBase by removing leading top-level `::` from constant paths where the cop already reports redundancy.

asi.hypothesis: Top-level redundant constant-base offenses are safe to fix by deleting the initial `::` token.
asi.learning: Visitor-based check_source can emit deterministic corrections by removing first two bytes of offending constant path when source starts with `::`.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly local token rewrites in remaining Style/Lint cops; postpone layout and no-op Ruby-version cops.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantRegexpConstructor by replacing `Regexp.new(/.../)` and `Regexp.compile(/.../)` with direct regexp literals.

asi.hypothesis: Constructor-to-literal regexp rewrites are safe when exactly one regexp-literal argument is present.
asi.learning: Whole-call replacement with the original regexp argument source produced correct output for plain and optioned literals (e.g., `/baz/i`) while preserving guarded exclusions (extra args).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting Style canonicalization cops with whole-expression or token-level rewrites; defer risky multiline/layout transforms.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/CommandLiteral by converting `%x(...)` command literals to backtick form when inner-backtick-safe.

asi.hypothesis: Delimiter-only rewrites on command literals are safe for default backticks style when inner content has no backticks.
asi.learning: Replacing opening and closing delimiter ranges (`%x`-form -> backticks) yielded stable corrections for current offense fixtures while preserving conservative inner-backtick guards.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with token/selector-style cops that avoid multiline structural rewrites; keep ambiguous expression transformations conservative.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/DoubleCopDisableDirective by merging multiple same-line `rubocop:disable`/`rubocop:todo` directives into a single comma-separated directive.

asi.hypothesis: Line-local disable-directive cleanup is safe as a pure comment-text rewrite starting at the first directive token.
asi.learning: Replacing the comment segment from first directive to line end with merged comma-separated directives matched fixtures for both disable and todo forms.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep prioritizing style cops with deterministic token-level rewrites; avoid uncertain parser-warning autocorrections unless bounded.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/TrailingCommaInAttributeDeclaration by removing the trailing comma token in attr_* declarations that swallow following method defs.

asi.hypothesis: Trailing-comma attr declaration offenses can be corrected with a single-token removal at the detected comma offset.
asi.learning: Using the scanned comma position after the last real attr arg produced precise comma-only removals and preserved surrounding formatting/body lines.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly local Style/Lint rewrites; avoid broad structural rewrites unless fixture coverage is expanded first.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/WhileUntilDo by deleting the `do` segment between multiline loop conditions and loop bodies.

asi.hypothesis: Multiline `while/until ... do` offenses can be corrected by removing the range from condition end to `do` keyword end.
asi.learning: Using predicate end + `do_keyword_loc` produced stable corrections across wrapped predicates, inline comments, and one-line body forms while preserving guardrails.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similar keyword-token removal cops before attempting larger structural rewrites.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/BlockComments by rewriting `=begin...=end` blocks into `#` line comments while preserving inner content lines.

asi.hypothesis: Block comment offenses can be safely autocorrected with whole-block replacement from `=begin` through matching `=end` into commented inner lines.
asi.learning: Line-offset based block replacement handled nested `=begin` text and preserved existing heredoc/__END__ skip behavior while producing stable fixture output.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with source-level literal/keyword cops that can be corrected via bounded range replacements before layout-heavy work.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/EmptyHeredoc by replacing empty heredoc literals with empty string literals.

asi.hypothesis: Empty heredoc offenses are safely fixable by replacing the full heredoc range (opening through closing delimiter) with `''`.
asi.learning: Using opening_loc start and closing_loc end gave deterministic whole-node replacements; fixture output normalizes adjacent blank lines accordingly.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep selecting cops with bounded range replacements and strong fixture determinism; avoid ambiguous parser-warning edits for now.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantInterpolationUnfreeze by replacing redundant `+@`/`dup` wrappers around interpolated strings with the interpolated string expression itself.

asi.hypothesis: Redundant unfreeze wrappers on interpolated strings can be safely autocorrected by replacing the full call node with its interpolated-string receiver.
asi.learning: Whole-call replacement using receiver source handled both regular and heredoc interpolation forms in fixtures while respecting Ruby >= 3.0 guardrails.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with localized string/regexp style rewrites and avoid large structural transforms unless fixture coverage is strengthened.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/VariableInterpolation by converting `#$var`/`#@ivar`/`#@@cvar` interpolation forms to braced expressions (`#{$var}`, etc.).

asi.hypothesis: Embedded-variable interpolation offenses are safe to fix with node-local replacement of variable token to `{var}` inside interpolation.
asi.learning: Visitor-based source cop can emit deterministic corrections by replacing variable-node ranges with braced forms; this covered globals/ivars/cvars and `$0` cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep focusing on localized interpolation/selector rewrites and defer complex layout or parser-warning-based transformations.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/RescueType by removing invalid rescue exception types from rescue clauses (falling back to bare `rescue` when none remain valid).

asi.hypothesis: Invalid exception types in rescue clauses can be safely corrected by rewriting only the exception-list segment after `rescue`.
asi.learning: Visitor-based check_source correction replacing `keyword_loc.end..last_exception.end` handled single-invalid-type fixtures cleanly and keeps valid exception handling behavior for mixed lists.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly bounded clause/token rewrites; defer complex layout and ambiguous parser-diagnostic cops.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/PerlBackrefs by replacing Perl-style backrefs and English aliases with `Regexp.last_match` expressions (including namespace-aware `::` prefix).

asi.hypothesis: Perl backreference offenses are safe to autocorrect with node-local replacement to `Regexp.last_match` forms and class/module namespace prefixing.
asi.learning: Visitor-based replacement of backref/global/numbered reference node ranges produced stable corrections, including namespace-prefixed replacements inside module scope.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue mining Style/Lint cops with direct token-expression substitutions; keep layout and ambiguous-parser rewrites deprioritized.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/CaseEquality by rewriting common `===` patterns to explicit forms (`include?` for ranges, `is_a?` for constant/self.class receivers).

asi.hypothesis: A bounded subset of `===` offenses can be safely autocorrected with full-expression rewrites to clearer `include?`/`is_a?` calls.
asi.learning: Combining receiver-shape checks (range-like parenthesized receiver, constants, and `self.class`) with whole-call replacement passed fixture autocorrects while leaving unsupported shapes unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue targeting cops with explicit receiver-shape rewrite rules and avoid broad transformations lacking deterministic replacement patterns.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/CollectionMethods by rewriting deprecated enumerable selectors (e.g., collect/inject/detect/member?) to configured preferred methods.

asi.hypothesis: Collection method preference offenses can be safely autocorrected as selector-only replacements when existing block/implicit-block guardrails already match RuboCop.
asi.learning: Replacing only `message_loc` with the configured preferred selector produced stable fixes for explicit blocks, block-pass forms, and symbol-arg implicit blocks.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue pursuing selector-level Style rewrites in remaining non-layout departments; keep layout batch for later.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantStringEscape by removing only redundant escape backslashes inside double-quoted string/interpolated-string segments.

asi.hypothesis: Redundant string escapes can be corrected safely by deleting just the backslash character at each flagged escape location while preserving string content.
asi.learning: Existing scanner already isolates redundant escapes with interpolation guards (`\#{`, `\#$`, `\#@`); adding one-char removals yielded stable fixture autocorrect output.
asi.next_action_hint: Apply the same one-char escape-removal strategy to regexp escapes where redundancy logic is already in place.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantRegexpEscape by removing redundant regexp escape backslashes while preserving meaningful escapes (char-class context and delimiter cases).

asi.hypothesis: Redundant regexp escapes can be safely autocorrected via one-character backslash deletion at already-detected offense positions.
asi.learning: Existing escape classifier already handled char-class and %r delimiter exceptions; adding targeted backslash removals produced stable corrected fixtures.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for additional selector/token-only Style/Lint autocorrections before tackling high-risk Layout transformations.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ArrayIntersect by rewriting supported intersection checks (`(a & b).any?/empty?/none?` and `a.intersection(b).any?/empty?/none?`) to `intersect?` equivalents.

asi.hypothesis: Current Style/ArrayIntersect offense shapes can be safely corrected with whole-expression replacement to `intersect?`/negated `intersect?` forms.
asi.learning: Deriving replacements from parsed receiver/argument nodes enabled deterministic rewrites for the already-detected pattern subset without broadening detection logic.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with low-branching Style/Lint cops that already identify narrow AST shapes suitable for direct replacement.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/CollectionQuerying by rewriting `count`-predicate/comparison patterns to enumerable predicates (`any?`, `none?`, `one?`, `many?`) while preserving block forms.

asi.hypothesis: Existing CollectionQuerying matches can be autocorrected safely by replacing the `count` selector and removing the trailing predicate/comparison segment.
asi.learning: Two-range correction (selector replacement + tail removal from count-call end to outer-call end) produced stable rewrites for supported forms and kept safe-nav exclusions intact.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue targeting expression-local rewrites in Style before entering Layout-heavy transformations.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/CollectionCompact by rewriting supported `reject/select/filter` nil-pruning forms to `compact`/`compact!` with block-range replacement.

asi.hypothesis: CollectionCompact offenses can be corrected safely by replacing from method selector through block/block-pass segment with `compact`/`compact!`.
asi.learning: Using block-aware correction end offsets (block node end for `{...}` forms; call end for block-pass forms) avoided dangling-token artifacts and matched fixture expectations.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly scoped selector+tail rewrites and avoid broad layout/formatting cops until easy Style/Lint wins diminish.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/FileRead by rewriting `File.open(...).read`, `File.open(..., &:read)`, and simple read block forms to `File.read(...)`/`File.binread(...)`.

asi.hypothesis: Current FileRead detection spans are sufficient for whole-expression replacement to File convenience readers without broadening pattern scope.
asi.learning: Returning explicit replacement text from existing pattern analyzers (including mode-aware `binread`) allowed deterministic full-span rewrites across chain, block-pass, and simple block forms.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep mining mode/selector-local Style cops (e.g., HashEachMethods limited selector swaps) before high-risk layout/autofix work.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/HashEachMethods by rewriting `keys.each`/`values.each` chains and unused-key/value `each` block forms to `each_key`/`each_value` with block-arg cleanup.

asi.hypothesis: HashEachMethods offense shapes can be corrected with localized selector rewrites plus targeted block-parameter segment removal for unused arg cases.
asi.learning: Dual correction strategy (keys/values chain span rewrite and each-block selector+arg-range edits) matched fixture expectations and kept existing safety guards unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly constrained style cops (e.g., FileWrite/HashConversion subset) before considering complex layout/autofix work.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/FileWrite by rewriting supported `File.open(...).write(...)` and simple block-write forms to `File.write(...)`/`File.binwrite(...)`.

asi.hypothesis: FileWrite offense spans can be safely replaced with convenience class methods when open-mode and write-argument shape checks already pass.
asi.learning: Mode-aware whole-span replacement using extracted filename/content sources worked for chain and block forms while respecting existing strict mode/arg guards.
asi.next_action_hint: Next promising path is guarded HashConversion autocorrect (to_h/literal forms) or conservative Lint candidate exploration before layout-heavy work.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/HashConversion by rewriting supported `Hash[...]` forms to `.to_h` or literal hash expressions while preserving nested-Hash suppression behavior.

asi.hypothesis: HashConversion offenses can be corrected from existing argument-shape checks with full-expression replacements (`to_h` / `{...}`) without expanding detection scope.
asi.learning: Visitor-level correction generation worked for single-arg, keyword-hash, empty, and even-arity multi-arg forms; odd-arity and splat-allowed cases remain offense-only/ignored as intended.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with conservative Style/Lint replacements (potentially ArrayFirstLast plain-read subset) and defer layout/autofmt-heavy cops.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/FileTouch by replacing `File.open(..., 'a') {}` empty-block append-touch forms with `FileUtils.touch(...)`.

asi.hypothesis: FileTouch offenses are safe whole-expression rewrites because the cop already constrains to empty append-mode blocks.
asi.learning: Using call-start to block-end replacement preserves argument expressions and yields deterministic touch rewrites on existing offense shapes.
asi.next_action_hint: Next low-risk path remains targeted Style rewrites; keep Lint/AmbiguousRegexpLiteral conservative and postpone layout-heavy fixes.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/FileEmpty by rewriting supported `zero?/size==0/read==''/read.empty?` file-emptiness checks to `File.empty?`/`FileTest.empty?` calls.

asi.hypothesis: Existing FileEmpty offense patterns can be corrected via full-expression replacement to class `.empty?(arg)` calls without widening detection scope.
asi.learning: Centralized offense reporter with replacement generation made all currently detected patterns autocorrectable while preserving receiver class (`File` vs `FileTest`).
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with conservative Style cop rewrites and keep parser-ambiguity/layout cops deferred unless a tightly bounded transform is proven.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/GlobalStdStream by replacing STDIN/STDOUT/STDERR constant references with corresponding `$stdin/$stdout/$stderr` globals in flagged contexts.

asi.hypothesis: GlobalStdStream offenses are direct token replacements, so correction can be safely applied at the constant location once assignment-context guards pass.
asi.learning: Visitor-level replacement at constant locations preserved existing write-target and assignment-context exclusions while making all offense fixtures autocorrect cleanly.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue harvesting direct token/selector Style fixes; postpone Layout and ambiguity-sensitive Lint rewrites.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/HashSlice by rewriting supported `select/filter` block patterns (`k == value` and `include?(k)`) to `slice(...)`/`slice(*...)`.

asi.hypothesis: Current HashSlice offense patterns can be corrected by replacing the selector-through-block segment with direct `slice` calls.
asi.learning: Block-end bounded replacement from `message_loc` handled both equality and include-pattern offenses cleanly while preserving receiver expressions.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue incremental Style rewrites with strict AST-shape guards; avoid broad layout and ambiguous-regex transforms for now.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/HashFetchChain by replacing chained `fetch(..., nil/{}/Hash.new)` segments with a single `dig(...)` call over collected keys.

asi.hypothesis: Existing HashFetchChain chain-walk metadata can drive a bounded selector-to-end replacement with equivalent `dig(...)` arguments.
asi.learning: Using outermost fetch selector offset plus terminal call end offset produced stable rewrites for 2- and 3-level chains, including multiline receivers.
asi.next_action_hint: Next candidates should remain AST-shape-local and token/selector bounded; keep deferring layout and high-ambiguity lint transforms.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/HashExcept by rewriting supported `reject/select/filter` key-comparison block patterns to direct `except(...)` calls.

asi.hypothesis: HashExcept offense shapes can use the same bounded selector-to-block replacement pattern as HashSlice with `except(...)` target calls.
asi.learning: Block-end replacement from call selector handled reject/select/filter comparison cases deterministically and matched fixture autocorrect output.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue similar hash-subset style cops and keep ambiguity/layout-heavy cops deferred.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/FetchEnvVar by replacing flagged `ENV[...]` accesses with `ENV.fetch(..., nil)` while preserving existing suppression logic for flag/comparison/guarded uses.

asi.hypothesis: FetchEnvVar offenses are safe node-local rewrites: every reported `ENV[...]` can be replaced with `ENV.fetch(...)` using configured nil-default behavior.
asi.learning: Adding correction emission in the existing visitor preserved complex suppression behavior (conditions, ||-lhs, predicates, quote-normalized key guards) while enabling deterministic autocorrect on offense fixtures.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly bounded Style cops where offense spans already map directly to replacement expressions; defer ambiguous parser/language semantics.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ArrayFirstLast on bracket-read offenses by rewriting `[0]`/`[-1]` (including explicit `.[]`/`&.[]` forms) to `first`/`last` while leaving index-write diagnostics offense-only.

asi.hypothesis: ArrayFirstLast read-style `[]` offenses can be corrected with bounded selector-to-call-end replacement, while index-write shapes should remain uncorrected for safety.
asi.learning: Using `message_loc..call.end` with operator-aware replacement (`.first`/`.last` vs `first`/`last` for explicit dot/safe-nav calls) produced stable corrections across chained and explicit bracket syntax.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with direct selector/block-tail rewrites in remaining Style cops; keep write-assignment and layout-heavy transformations conservative.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/NegativeArrayIndex by rewriting `arr[arr.length/size/count - n]` and supported range-end subtraction forms to negative-index equivalents.

asi.hypothesis: NegativeArrayIndex offense ranges already isolate index/range expressions that can be safely replaced with `-n` and `..-n`/`...-n` forms.
asi.learning: Applying corrections at argument-range granularity preserved receiver/method chains and produced stable rewrites for both simple and parenthesized range patterns.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting style cops with explicit expression-span replacements and avoid broad layout or ambiguous parse rewrites.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/NestedFileDirname by collapsing nested `File.dirname(...)` chains into single `dirname(path, level)` selector-tail rewrites.

asi.hypothesis: NestedFileDirname offenses can be safely corrected by replacing from outer `dirname` selector through call end with computed `dirname(inner, depth)` form.
asi.learning: Existing nesting analysis already computes innermost path and level, so adding selector-tail replacement yielded deterministic autocorrect across 2-level and 3-level nests.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with other selector-tail style rewrites where message already provides exact replacement shape.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/HashAsLastArrayItem by wrapping unbraced trailing hash elements in braces (and unwrapping in no_braces mode when applicable).

asi.hypothesis: HashAsLastArrayItem offenses map to localized last-element rewrites, enabling safe node-span corrections without altering array structure.
asi.learning: Direct last-node replacement (`{ ... }` wrapping in braces mode, brace stripping in no_braces mode) passed fixture autocorrect while preserving existing skip guards (kwsplat/all-hash/adjacent-hash).
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with expression-local Style rewrites and maintain conservative handling for layout and ambiguous-lint domains.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/OrAssignment by rewriting detected `x = x || y` and `x = x ? x : y` patterns to `x ||= y`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing OrAssignment offense patterns provide enough AST context to construct direct `||=` replacements over full assignment spans.
asi.learning: Assignment-span replacement using RHS extraction from `or` and ternary else branches produced stable autocorrect fixture output without broadening match logic.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly deterministic assignment/selector rewrites in remaining Style cops before touching layout-heavy classes.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/NumericLiteralPrefix by rewriting uppercase/bare-prefix integer literals (`0X`,`0B`,`0D/0d`,`0O`, bare octal) to canonical forms while preserving sign handling and complex/rational skips.

asi.hypothesis: NumericLiteralPrefix offenses can be safely autocorrected with full-literal replacement derived from existing prefix classification logic.
asi.learning: Using literal-local rewrites that preserve sign prefixes (`+/-`) and apply one canonical prefix transform per offense matched fixture output and respected existing guards for underscore-decimals and trailing i/r literals.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with deterministic selector/literal rewrites in Style before tackling Layout or ambiguity-sensitive Lint cops.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ClassMethods by replacing class/module-name receivers in `def ClassName.method` with `self`; add corrected fixture coverage and prune stale autoresearch ideas.

asi.hypothesis: ClassMethods offenses are safe receiver-token rewrites because the cop already verifies receiver name equals enclosing class/module.
asi.learning: Receiver-range replacement to `self` in def nodes produced stable fixture autocorrect output while preserving singleton-class suppression logic.
asi.ideas_pruned: Removed stale generic file-name/refactor items and refreshed backlog toward concrete safe candidates.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with hash transform cops or similarly bounded selector/block-tail rewrites; keep ambiguity/layout transformations conservative.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/HashTransformKeys by rewriting detected `each_with_object({})` key-transform patterns to `transform_keys { |k| ... }`.

asi.hypothesis: HashTransformKeys offenses are safe whole-call rewrites because current detection already enforces destructured hash params and unchanged value pass-through.
asi.learning: Reconstructing replacement from receiver source + extracted key expression yielded stable `transform_keys` autocorrect output for current offense fixtures without broadening detection scope.
asi.next_action_hint: Attempt Style/HashTransformValues next using the same conservative whole-call replacement strategy per matched pattern variant.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/HashTransformValues by rewriting detected `each_with_object`, `Hash[map]`, `map.to_h`, and `to_h {}` value-transform patterns to `transform_values { |v| ... }`.

asi.hypothesis: HashTransformValues offense shapes can be safely autocorrected as whole-expression rewrites because existing pattern checks already enforce unchanged keys and transformed values.
asi.learning: Returning block extraction metadata (value arg/value expr) enabled deterministic replacements across all four detected variants, including multiline map/Hash[] forms.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly constrained Style cops (e.g., method/selector preference rewrites) before moving into high-risk layout/autofmt transformations.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ExactRegexpMatch by rewriting exact anchored literal-regexp comparisons (`=~`, `!~`, `===`, `match`, `match?`) to direct string equality/inequality checks.

asi.hypothesis: ExactRegexpMatch offenses already guarantee anchored literal patterns, so whole-expression replacement to `==`/`!=` string comparisons is safe and deterministic.
asi.learning: Extracting the literal from `\A...\z` and escaping it for single-quoted Ruby strings produced stable autocorrect output across operator and `match` forms.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with Style cops that have explicit one-expression canonical forms and existing strict pattern guards.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ClassEqualityComparison by rewriting flagged class-equality comparisons to `instance_of?` (including conservative string-class-name conversion for `.class.name` forms), and refresh autoresearch docs/ideas.

asi.hypothesis: ClassEqualityComparison offenses can be converted with full-expression replacement when receiver class extraction is explicit and RHS class argument is safely derivable.
asi.learning: Building replacements from the underlying object receiver plus RHS class argument worked for direct class comparisons and safe `.class.name == 'Const'` cases while leaving ambiguous RHS shapes offense-only.
asi.ideas_pruned: Removed completed hash-transform items and refocused backlog on remaining conservative Style/Lint/Security candidates.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with small expression-local Style rewrites; keep Layout backlog staged and ambiguity-sensitive lint transforms conservative.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ExpandPathArguments by rewriting flagged `File.expand_path(<literal>, __FILE__)` calls to the cop’s normalized `__dir__`/path form with whole-call replacement.

asi.hypothesis: ExpandPathArguments offenses are safe full-call rewrites because the cop already restricts to literal path + `__FILE__` two-arg `File.expand_path` patterns.
asi.learning: Reusing the existing suggestion builder and replacing the full send node (`receiver.suggestion`) produced stable corrected fixtures for current offense scope.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with conservative expression-local Style rewrites; keep security/lint ambiguity candidates gated behind strict semantic checks.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/EmptyClassDefinition (class_definition style) by rewriting `Const = Class.new([Parent])` offenses to two-line `class Const < Parent` / `class Const` forms.

asi.hypothesis: EmptyClassDefinition offenses can be safely fixed with whole-assignment replacement because detection already constrains to blockless `Class.new` constant assignments with allowed parent forms.
asi.learning: Deriving class name from assignment LHS and optional parent from `Class.new` argument produced stable corrections for simple, namespaced, absolute, and indented constant targets.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly explicit assignment/call canonicalizations; keep layout and semantic-risk cops deferred.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ModuleMemberExistenceCheck by replacing `*_methods/constants/... .include?/member?` chains with direct predicate calls (`method_defined?`, `const_defined?`, etc.), preserving inherit-argument handling.

asi.hypothesis: ModuleMemberExistenceCheck offenses are safe selector-to-tail rewrites because current matcher already constrains to simple single-argument include/member checks.
asi.learning: Replacing from inner method selector through outer call end with predicate calls (including optional inherit arg passthrough except explicit `true`) matched fixture behavior and RuboCop shape.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similar one-expression style canonicalizations; avoid metric-only toggles like no-op YAMLLoad support.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantFormat by replacing redundant `format/sprintf` calls with direct literal/constant expressions and computed literal strings for supported simple format-specifier cases.

asi.hypothesis: RedundantFormat offenses already isolate deterministic literal-only `format/sprintf` calls, so full-call replacement can safely preserve semantics for the supported formatter subset.
asi.learning: Using the cop’s existing format-spec parser/renderer as the replacement source allowed stable autocorrect for single-arg redundant calls and simple multi-arg `%s/%d/%f` literal patterns.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with style cops that already compute a canonical preferred expression internally; keep high-ambiguity lint/layout cops deferred.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantFetchBlock by replacing redundant `fetch(key) { literal/default }` block forms (including scenario fixtures) with direct `fetch(key, default)` calls.

asi.hypothesis: RedundantFetchBlock offenses already compute stable key/default expressions, so selector-to-block-end replacement can safely produce direct `fetch` default-argument calls.
asi.learning: Using custom scenario autocorrect fixtures confirmed stable rewrites for literal defaults, frozen-string guarded cases, and unary-minus literal spacing (`- 1`) without widening detection.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with strict-subset Style/EachWithObject autocorrect if block-param swap + trailing accumulator removal can be bounded safely.

description: Enable conservative autocorrect for Style/EachWithObject by rewriting flagged `inject/reduce` selectors to `each_with_object`, swapping two required block params, and removing trailing accumulator return expressions.

asi.hypothesis: For existing EachWithObject offenses, a bounded three-edit transform (selector swap, param swap, trailing accumulator removal) is sufficient to reach valid canonical form.
asi.learning: Restricting autocorrect to blocks with exactly two required parameters avoided unsafe rewrites while matching fixture behavior for brace and do/end blocks.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for next style cops with similarly explicit canonical replacements and low overlap risk before entering layout-heavy backlog.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ZeroLengthPredicate by rewriting flagged `.length/.size` zero/nonzero checks (`zero?`, `== 0`, `< 1`, `!= 0`, `> 0`, and swapped forms) to `empty?`/`!empty?`.

asi.hypothesis: ZeroLengthPredicate offenses can be safely converted to `empty?` predicates when existing guards already exclude safe-navigation and non-collection receivers.
asi.learning: Centralizing replacement derivation from the underlying `.length/.size` receiver yielded deterministic full-expression rewrites across direct and swapped comparison forms.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with style cops that already compute a preferred predicate/expression and only need bounded correction emission.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ReverseFind (Ruby >= 4.0) by rewriting `reverse.find`/`reverse_each.find|detect` selector spans to `rfind`, with config-aware autocorrect fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: ReverseFind offenses are safe selector-span rewrites because existing detection already constrains to no-arg reverse/reverse_each + find/detect with compatible block forms.
asi.learning: Replacing from inner receiver selector through outer method selector (`reverse...find` -> `rfind`) preserved multiline chains and block forms while respecting Ruby version gating.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with style cops that expose deterministic selector/tail replacement ranges and explicit guard conditions.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantFilterChain by rewriting `select/filter/find_all` selector to the target predicate (`any?`/`none?`/`one?`/`many?`) and removing trailing predicate-chain call.

asi.hypothesis: RedundantFilterChain offenses can be corrected with two localized edits because existing matching already isolates filter-selector and trailing predicate positions.
asi.learning: Pairing selector replacement on the filter call with removal of `.predicate` chain segment produced stable autocorrect output while preserving existing it/numblock and ActiveSupport guards.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly bounded style chain canonicalizations; keep ambiguity-sensitive lint and layout work staged.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ModuleFunction by rewriting `extend self` to `module_function` under module_function style while preserving existing private-directive and style guards.

asi.hypothesis: ModuleFunction offenses are safe whole-call rewrites in module_function style because existing detection already identifies plain `extend self` directives in eligible module bodies.
asi.learning: Adding correction emission at the directive call range (`extend self` -> `module_function`) produced stable fixture autocorrect output without changing existing style/private exclusion behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with style cops where detection already computes canonical replacement forms and edits can stay call-local.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ComparableClamp by rewriting matched `if/elsif/else` clamp patterns to `x.clamp(min, max)` full-expression replacements.

asi.hypothesis: ComparableClamp offenses already encode enough branch-shape information to derive unambiguous `(x, min, max)` parts and replace the whole conditional safely.
asi.learning: Extracting per-condition bound kind (min vs max) from comparator orientation/body alignment enabled robust clamp reconstruction across operand-order variants.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with branch/selector-local style cops where canonical replacement text can be computed directly from matched AST parts.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ComparableBetween by rewriting matched logical bound comparisons (`x >= min && x <= max` variants) to `x.between?(min, max)` full-expression replacements.

asi.hypothesis: ComparableBetween offenses can be safely corrected when comparison normalization identifies a shared value with distinct min/max bounds.
asi.learning: Normalizing each comparison into small/big sides allowed deterministic extraction of `(value, min, max)` across operand-order variants for direct `between?` replacement.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting arithmetic/comparison style cops with similarly explicit canonical replacements before moving into layout-heavy backlog.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/MapToHash by rewriting `map/collect ... .to_h` chains to `to_h` with block pass-through, including destructured block-arg normalization.

asi.hypothesis: MapToHash offenses are safe chain-local rewrites because existing detection already constrains to `map/collect` feeding a no-arg `to_h` call with compatible block forms.
asi.learning: Applying two span edits (inner selector `map/collect` -> `to_h`, remove trailing `.to_h`) plus optional `|(k,v)|` destructuring unwrapping produced stable autocorrect output for literal and symbol block-pass forms.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with style chain canonicalizations that have explicit selector/tail replacement boundaries and no additional semantic inference.

description: Coordinate non-overlap by implementing autocorrect for Lint/RedundantDirGlobSort (remove `.sort` after Dir.glob/Dir[] on Ruby >=3.0) while including concurrent Style/NilLambda autocorrect work already present in tree; added corrected fixtures and fixture autocorrect tests.

asi.hypothesis: Taking an unclaimed low-risk Lint selector-span removal cop should reduce the core autocorrect gap without overlapping the other autoresearcher's active Style cop.
asi.coordination: Avoided touching Style/NilLambda and implemented Lint/RedundantDirGlobSort instead.

asi.learning: Removing from call operator through `sort` selector (`.sort`) is sufficient and deterministic for redundant sort offenses.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue claiming distinct cops from the remaining list (prefer Lint/Style token/selector rewrites not currently in-flight by the other instance).

description: Add autocorrect support for Style/NilLambda (remove nil-only lambda/proc bodies) and include Lint/RedundantDirGlobSort selector-tail cleanup from current tree, reducing remaining core autocorrect gap.

asi.hypothesis: Nil-return lambda/proc offenses can be corrected by deleting only the nil body span (with surrounding-space/line handling), while retaining original lambda/proc delimiters.
asi.learning: Body-range deletion with single-line whitespace trimming and multi-line whole-line removal produced stable empty lambda/proc forms and closed an additional gap in current tree state.
asi.next_action_hint: Prioritize remaining chain/selector cops where detection already identifies canonical replacement spans; avoid semantics-heavy layout rewrites for now.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/DeprecatedClassMethods with targeted replacements (`exists?`→`exist?`, `ENV.clone/dup`→`to_h`, `ENV.freeze`→receiver, `iterator?`→`block_given?`, `attr ... true/false`→`attr_accessor/reader`), while leaving Socket variants offense-only.

asi.hypothesis: DeprecatedClassMethods can safely autocorrect via selector/full-call rewrites for non-socket patterns because replacement text is fully determined by existing match groups.
asi.learning: Keeping Socket offenses non-corrected (RuboCop parity) while correcting the deterministic ENV/File/Dir/iterator?/attr cases improved overlap without broad semantic edits.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with cops where message-time preferred form is directly computable from matched node spans and avoid transformations requiring dataflow reasoning.

description: Stay on a separate Lint lane from the other researcher and add conservative autocorrect for Lint/DeprecatedClassMethods (`exists?`->`exist?`, `ENV.clone/dup`->`to_h`, `ENV.freeze`->`ENV`, `iterator?`->`block_given?`, `attr` boolean forms -> accessor/reader), leaving Socket rewrites offense-only.

asi.hypothesis: A conservative subset autocorrect for DeprecatedClassMethods should yield a safe -1 without overlapping in-flight Style work.

asi.learning: Message-loc selector rewrites and full-call replacement for `attr`/`ENV.freeze` cover most deprecated forms safely; socket deprecations are best left offense-only for now.

description: Resume loop with a separate Lint track: implement autocorrect for Lint/RedundantRequireStatement by removing redundant `require` statements (including trailing newline handling), while picking up concurrent in-tree Style/CommentAnnotation autocorrect work from the other instance.

description: Advance the lint/style autocorrect lane by adding conservative autocorrect for Lint/RedundantRequireStatement and Style/CommentAnnotation (normalize annotation prefix formatting only when a note exists).

asi.learning: Keeping CommentAnnotation corrections limited to the annotation-prefix span (and skipping no-note cases) mirrors RuboCop behavior while avoiding broad comment rewrites.

description: Continue non-overlapping Lint lane by adding autocorrect for Lint/DuplicateSetElement via conservative set-element list rewrite that removes repeated literal/const/var elements in Set[]/Set.new([...])/[...].to_set forms with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Duplicate set literal elements can be safely autocorrected by rebuilding the element list with first occurrences only, avoiding per-token comma surgery.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/DuplicateSetElement by deduplicating repeated literal/constant/variable elements inside set literals/constructors and replacing the element span with first-occurrence order preserved.

asi.hypothesis: DuplicateSetElement offenses have a deterministic fix: rebuild the set element list by retaining first occurrences and dropping repeated literal/const/var entries.
asi.learning: Element-span replacement (first..last element only) provides stable autocorrect across `Set[]`, `Set.new([...])`, and `[...].to_set` forms while preserving non-literal elements.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/DuplicateRequire by safely removing duplicate standalone `require`/`require_relative` lines (including `Kernel.require`) within the same parent scope.

asi.hypothesis: DuplicateRequire can be safely autocorrected with full-line deletion when the duplicate call is a standalone statement in its line, preserving wrappers and mixed-expression cases offense-only.

description: Continue resumed loop on non-overlapping Lint path: add autocorrect for Lint/RequireRelativeSelfPath by removing self-require lines; include concurrent in-tree Lint/DuplicateRequire autocorrect and ideas backlog refresh.

asi.learning: For line-oriented redundant require calls, deleting call span plus newline yields clean corrected output and stable fixture behavior.
asi.ideas_update: Added deferred idea for conservative statement-parent-only Lint/DuplicateRequire autocorrect boundary checks.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting unclaimed Lint cops with deterministic localized rewrites before moving to higher-risk Layout transformations.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/TripleQuotes by removing empty adjacent string fragments produced by multi-quote delimiters, collapsing offenses like `"""foo"""`/`'''bar'''` to normal single-delimited strings.

asi.hypothesis: TripleQuotes offenses can be corrected by deleting only empty string child nodes in the interpolated-string AST, which preserves actual content while removing redundant quote fragments.
asi.learning: Prism exposes triple-quote artifacts as empty string parts; removing those ranges (while keeping one when all parts are empty) yields stable canonical literals without touching heredocs/regexes.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep prioritizing lint cops with correction ranges tied to explicit redundant AST fragments (token/list pruning) before complex flow/layout cops.

description: Resume loop on non-overlapping Lint lane by adding autocorrect for Lint/RedundantTypeConversion (remove redundant `.to_s/.to_sym/.to_i/...` selector spans) and include concurrent in-tree Lint/TripleQuotes autocorrect work.

asi.hypothesis: Redundant type conversion offenses can be safely autocorrected by deleting only the outer call operator+selector range, preserving receiver expression text.
asi.coordination: Kept to Lint cops to avoid overlap with the other researcher’s Style/Layout work.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::redundant_type_conversion && cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::triple_quotes
asi.learning: Operator-to-selector span deletion (`call_operator_loc..message_loc`) is a reliable pattern for no-arg redundant conversion cops.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with remaining Lint cops that can use localized selector/statement rewrites; defer ambiguous semantic/layout edits.
